query: Which NIST AI RMF functions should a team use to establish AI risk context and then check whether the risks are being tracked quantitatively?

context.title: NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0
context.text: The AI RMF organizes risk management around four functions. Govern establishes policies, accountability, and organizational practices. Map frames the intended use, context, stakeholders, and risks. Measure analyzes, assesses, benchmarks, and monitors risks and related impacts. Manage prioritizes and acts on mapped and measured risks.

context.title: NIST AI RMF Playbook
context.text: The playbook expands the RMF functions into suggested actions and documentation prompts. It is meant to help teams operationalize the Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage functions for concrete AI systems.

expected_answer: Use Map to establish the AI system context and Measure to analyze, assess, benchmark, and monitor whether risks are being tracked quantitatively. Govern supports both by setting accountability and policy.

query: For a product memo, summarize the EU AI Act's high-level risk posture and name the class of AI practices that receive the strictest treatment.

context.title: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 Artificial Intelligence Act
context.text: The EU AI Act creates a risk-based framework for AI systems in the Union. The framework distinguishes unacceptable-risk practices, high-risk systems, transparency obligations for certain AI systems, and rules for general-purpose AI models. Unacceptable-risk AI practices are prohibited rather than merely documented.

context.title: European Commission AI Act Overview
context.text: The Commission describes the AI Act as a regulatory framework that classifies AI systems by risk and applies stronger obligations as potential harm increases. Some practices are banned, while high-risk systems face requirements around risk management, data, documentation, transparency, human oversight, accuracy, and security.

expected_answer: The EU AI Act is risk-based: obligations increase with potential harm. The strictest category is unacceptable-risk AI practices, which are prohibited.

query: What governance themes from the 2023 U.S. AI executive order should be cited when explaining why frontier-model safety tests and agency guidance matter?

context.title: Executive Order 14110 on Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy AI
context.text: Executive Order 14110 directed federal agencies to advance safe, secure, and trustworthy AI. It emphasized safety testing, standards, privacy, civil rights, consumer and worker protection, innovation, competition, and federal government use of AI. The order treated standards and agency guidance as mechanisms for reducing AI-related risk.

context.title: NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0
context.text: The NIST AI RMF provides a voluntary framework for managing AI risks. It centers organizational governance, mapping context, measuring risks, and managing prioritized risks over the AI lifecycle.

expected_answer: Cite the order's focus on safe, secure, and trustworthy AI, especially safety testing and standards, plus agency guidance on privacy, civil rights, consumer and worker protection, innovation, competition, and responsible government use.
